evals.prompt: In a Vite React TypeScript application, add a reusable profile badge component, a hook that loads the current profile, a pure utility that formats the display name, and tests for each unit. The project already has src/components, src/hooks, src/utils, and src/tests. Produce the complete proposed files under the requested output directory, including source and tests, and include a short SUMMARY.md explaining placement decisions. Do not modify any real repository files outside the output directory.
evals.expected_output: Working React TypeScript source files organized by responsibility, using kebab-case names, named exports, and tests mirrored under src/tests.
expectations: The output creates substantive source files at src/components/profile-badge.tsx, src/hooks/use-current-profile.ts, and src/utils/format-profile-name.ts or equivalently specific kebab-case names under those exact responsibility directories.
Unit and component tests are placed under src/tests and mirror the source responsibility paths, including tests/components, tests/hooks, and tests/utils.
All authored file and directory names use kebab-case except recognized names such as index.ts.
New components, hooks, and utilities use named exports by default, and tests consume those named exports.
The output does not introduce a features directory, unrelated empty directories, or unrelated project migration.
The produced files contain working React-TypeScript and test implementations rather than empty placeholders or prose-only suggestions.

evals.prompt: In an existing React TypeScript application, implement a reusable file viewer component. It needs a private toolbar subcomponent, a component-specific hook, public prop types, a pure formatting helper, component-specific CSS Module styles, and tests. Produce the complete proposed files under the requested output directory and include a short SUMMARY.md explaining the public API and file placement. Do not modify any real repository files outside the output directory.
evals.expected_output: A complex component in a same-name directory with a narrow public index, private companion modules, colocated CSS Module styles, and mirrored tests.
expectations: The output creates a same-name kebab-case directory at src/components/file-viewer containing file-viewer.tsx and index.ts.
The complex component directory contains separate substantive files for a private toolbar, component-specific hook, public types, pure utility, and CSS Module using kebab-case names.
The index.ts file exposes only the public FileViewer API and public prop types; it does not export the private toolbar, private hook, private utility, or styles.
Tests are placed under src/tests/components/file-viewer and exercise the component public API rather than being colocated beside source files.
The component and its public types use named exports by default, and external-style test imports use the directory public entry rather than deep-importing private implementation files.
The output does not introduce a features directory, broad application barrel, unrelated empty directories, or unrelated migration.

evals.prompt: Extend an existing Vite React TypeScript dashboard with an audit log page, a React Router route at /audit, a Zustand store for cross-page audit filters, page-specific styling, and unit tests. Assume the existing source tree already contains src/components/ui, src/pages, src/routes, src/stores, src/styles, and src/tests. Produce only the files required for this feature under the requested output directory, plus a short SUMMARY.md. Do not reorganize unrelated project areas and do not modify any real repository files outside the output directory.
evals.expected_output: A minimal audit-log extension with separate page, route, store, style, and mirrored test files using kebab-case paths and named exports.
expectations: The output creates a kebab-case audit page under src/pages, an audit route definition under src/routes, and a Zustand audit filter store under src/stores.
Route construction is kept in src/routes and imports the page; the page does not own the router configuration or import route internals merely to render UI.
Page and store tests are placed under mirrored src/tests/pages and src/tests/stores paths rather than beside source files.
The page, route, and store use named exports by default unless a documented router API requires otherwise.
Page-specific styling is authored with a kebab-case filename and kept with a clear page or styles owner rather than placed in an unrelated component directory.
The output changes only audit-feature files or a necessary routes public entry, and does not introduce features, reorganize unrelated areas, or create empty directories.

evals.prompt: Extend a Vite React TypeScript application with a reports page, a React Router reports route, a shared report formatter, and mirrored tests. The existing tsconfig.json contains compilerOptions.baseUrl set to '.' and compilerOptions.paths with '@/*' mapped to ['src/*']; Vite and Vitest already resolve the same alias. Nearby files contain a mixture of relative and aliased cross-directory imports. Produce complete files under the requested output directory without changing configuration or unrelated files, and include a short SUMMARY.md explaining the import strategy.
evals.expected_output: Kebab-case page, route, utility, and mirrored test files whose cross-directory imports use the configured @/ alias while same-directory imports remain relative.
expectations: Cross-directory imports in source files use the configured @/ alias, including the reports route importing the reports page and the page importing the shared formatter when applicable.
Mirrored tests import source modules through @/ paths rather than traversing from src/tests with ../ segments.
Imports between files inside the same owning directory may remain short relative imports such as ./module.
The output does not add or modify tsconfig, Vite, Vitest, or other alias configuration because the alias is already fully configured.
The summary explicitly says that @/ was selected from the existing tsconfig paths mapping rather than invented from convention.
The output preserves the skill's kebab-case naming, responsibility directories, mirrored tests, and named-export rules.

evals.prompt: Extend a Vite React TypeScript application with a settings page, a React Router settings route, a shared settings formatter, and mirrored tests. The existing tsconfig.json has no baseUrl or paths mappings, and the project has no bundler or test-runner alias configuration. Produce complete files under the requested output directory without adding an alias or changing unrelated configuration, and include a short SUMMARY.md explaining the import strategy.
evals.expected_output: Kebab-case page, route, utility, and mirrored test files that correctly fall back to relative imports without inventing an @ alias.
expectations: Cross-directory imports use valid relative paths, including the settings route importing the settings page and mirrored tests importing source modules.
The output contains no @/ imports because tsconfig and the surrounding tooling do not define that alias.
The output does not add or modify tsconfig, Vite, Vitest, or other configuration merely to introduce an alias.
The summary explicitly says relative imports are the fallback because no alias is configured.
The output preserves the skill's kebab-case naming, responsibility directories, mirrored tests, and named-export rules.
